2606.16808.title: Adaptive and Explicit safe: Triggering Latent Safety Awareness in Large Reasoning Models
2606.16808.paper_summary: While Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) excel at complex tasks, they remain highly vulnerable to sophisticated jailbreaks and direct harmful queries. To address this vulnerability, prior works depend heavily on external manual data annotation for safety alignment. However, we observe that LRMs can inherently identify safety risks when being re-presented with original queries alongside their own reasoning trajectories -- a capability we term Latent Safety Awareness. To leverage this safety awareness, we first employ Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) to explicitly induce safe tags to trigger safety analysis and guidance following the initial reasoning content for unsafe queries, while preserving standard responses for general queries to ensure adaptive triggering. Subsequently, we apply Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to further enhance the correctness and stability of the safety analysis and guidance. Notably, responses required for both training stages are entirely generated by models being optimized. With (Safe Trigger) SFT and DPO, experimental results demonstrate significant safety enhancement. For example, the Attack Success Rate (ASR) of DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, on average, drops 24.65% and 36.72% on harmful and jailbreak benchmarks, respectively. Finally, our Safe Trigger method exerts almost no negative impact on general performance or user experience.
2606.16808.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型推理模型（LRMs）在複雜任務中表現出色，但它們仍然對高級越獄和直接有害查詢高度脆弱。為了解決這一脆弱性，先前的研究在安全對齊方面過度依賴外部手動數據標註。然而，我們觀察到，當LRMs重新呈現原始查詢及其自身的推理軌跡時，它們可以內在地識別安全風險——這一能力我們稱之為潛在安全意識。為了利用這種安全意識，我們首先採用監督式微調（SFT）來明確引入安全標籤，以觸發對不安全查詢的安全分析和指導，同時保留對一般查詢的標準回應以確保自適應觸發。隨後，我們應用直接偏好優化（DPO）進一步增強安全分析和指導的正確性和穩定性。值得注意的是，兩個訓練階段所需的回應完全由正在優化的模型生成。通過（安全觸發）SFT和DPO，實驗結果顯示出顯著的安全增強。例如，DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B的攻擊成功率（ASR）在有害和越獄基準上平均分別下降24.65%和36.72%。最後，我們的安全觸發方法對一般性能或用戶體驗幾乎沒有負面影響。
2606.16808.author: Ke Miao et.al.
2606.16808.authors: Ke Miao, Jiaxin Li, Hongliang Chen, Yuke Hu, Zhan Qin
